conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text: Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. Let me first review the official eligibility rules so I can guide you properly.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for your patience. To help determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit, I need to ask you some questions.

First, do you have any children, and if so, how many?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I have one child. His name is Alex, and he's 8 years old.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. Now I need to ask you a few more questions about your situation with Alex.

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Great, thank you. Next question:

Do you live with Alex, or does he live with someone else?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex doesn't live with me - he lives with someone else.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
I understand. Since Alex doesn't live with you, I need to ask:

Do you make any financial contributions towards Alex's upkeep (such as paying for food, clothes, pocket money, or contributing to his accommodation)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do contribute towards his upkeep. I help out with things like clothes, food, and other things he needs.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. To assess your eligibility properly, I need to know:

How much do you contribute towards Alex's upkeep, and how often do you make these contributions (for example, weekly, monthly, or in lump sums)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: I contribute weekly. I give £26.05 each week towards his upkeep.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing that detail. Just a couple more questions:

Is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you. One final question:

Is Alex currently in hospital or care for an extended period, or does he have any special circumstances I should know about (such as being in approved education if he were older)?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, Alex isn't in hospital or care or anything like that. He's just a regular 8-year-old living his normal life.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. Let me now assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me:

- You have one child, Alex, who is 8 years old
- You live in the UK
- Alex lives with someone else (not with you)
- You contribute £26.05 per week towards his upkeep
- Nobody else is currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex
- Alex is not in hospital or care

According to the official rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You're usually considered responsible for a child if you're paying at least the same amount as Child Benefit (or the equivalent in kind) towards looking after them - for example on food, clothes or pocket money.

The current Child Benefit rate for an only or eldest child is £25.60 per week. Since you're contributing £26.05 per week towards Alex's upkeep, which is more than the Child Benefit amount, you meet the responsibility test even though Alex doesn't live with you.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Even though Alex doesn't live with you, your weekly contribution of £26.05 towards his upkeep is sufficient to establish you as being responsible for him, as it exceeds the Child Benefit rate. Since you live in the UK, Alex is under 16, and nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him, you meet all the eligibility requirements.

You can proceed with making a claim for Child Benefit. Just remember to continue making those regular contributions towards Alex's upkeep, as this is what establishes your responsibility for him under the rules.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 8 years old and does not live with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 8 (under 16)
  - Claimant contributes £26.05/week towards child's upkeep (>= £26.05) and no other claimaint has priority
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

Alex is not in hospital or care. Therefore, the claimant meets all eligibility criteria for Child Benefit for Alex.
